title: $NO_DEBUG
text: $NO_DEBUG Determine whether to compile components, application shells, and global ProcScripts that can be debugged, $NO_DEBUG { = } 1 | 0 Defaults Assignment file: ide.asn Section: [SETTINGS] Default value: 0 Description When $NO_DEBUG = 1 , compiling components, application shells, and global ProcScripts generates corresponding runtime objects that cannot be debugged. This is useful when preparing an application for release. It is the equivalent of using the /nodebug command line switch. Note: The DebugPort and EnableDevTools properties are ignored or disabled in case a component is compiled with /nodebug. Related Links /nodebug Instruct Uniface to compile the application shell, component, or global ProcScript so that it cannot be debugged. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
